title: `buildah run` won't start the container
body:
## Problem

When run `buildah run` the process remains stuck for very long time, when stopped using `Ctrl + C` then I get error `/bin/bash: line 1: $'\r': command not found`.

```console
# ctrid_fedora=$(buildah from fedora)
# buildah run $ctrid_fedora /bin/bash
^C
# /bin/bash: line 1: $'\r': command not found
```


## Version

```console
# buildah -v
buildah version 0.4 (image-spec 1.0.0, runtime-spec 1.0.0)
```

```console
# cat /etc/os-release 
NAME=Fedora
VERSION="26 (Cloud Edition)"
ID=fedora
VERSION_ID=26
PRETTY_NAME="Fedora 26 (Cloud Edition)"
ANSI_COLOR="0;34"
CPE_NAME="cpe:/o:fedoraproject:fedora:26"
HOME_URL="https://fedoraproject.org/"
BUG_REPORT_URL="https://bugzilla.redhat.com/"
REDHAT_BUGZILLA_PRODUCT="Fedora"
REDHAT_BUGZILLA_PRODUCT_VERSION=26
REDHAT_SUPPORT_PRODUCT="Fedora"
REDHAT_SUPPORT_PRODUCT_VERSION=26
PRIVACY_POLICY_URL=https://fedoraproject.org/wiki/Legal:PrivacyPolicy
VARIANT="Cloud Edition"
VARIANT_ID=cloud
```

comments.body: You did hit return after running the buildah run command, a couple of times?

```
# rpm -Uhv buildah-0.4-1.git9cbccf8.fc26.x86_64.rpm 
Preparing...                          ################################# [100%]
Updating / installing...
   1:buildah-0.4-1.git9cbccf8.fc26    ################################# [ 50%]
Cleaning up / removing...
   2:buildah-0.3-2.gitb9b2a8a.fc26    ################################# [100%]
# id=$(buildah from fedora)
# buildah run $id bash
# id
uid=0(root) gid=0(root) groups=0(root)
# exit
```
Works fine on my F26 box.

@surajssd Also make sure you have the latest runc installed.

dnf -y update runc --enablerepo updates-testing

Just to be sure, after you did:
'buildah run $ctrid_fedora /bin/bash'

You never got a reponse back right?  As @rhatdan asked, please update your runc to make sure that's OK.  If you're still having problems, please post the version of runc  that you're using.

FWIW, here's the output from my quick test this morning.

```
[root@localhost ~]# ctrid_fedora=$(buildah from fedora)
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob sha256:00ddb097f3f5c1afe2bc6bc6af1ffb9d2f75b300709f69cf02ded0c66318848e

Copying config sha256:b0b140824a486ccc0f7968f3c6ceb6982b4b77e82ef8b4faaf2806049fc266df
 0 B / 2.29 KiB [--------------------------------------------------------------]
 75.60 MiB / 75.60 MiB [=======================================================]Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
[root@localhost ~]# buildah run $ctrid_fedora /bin/bash
[root@mrsdalloway /]# exit
exit
[root@localhost ~]# runc --version
runc version 1.0.0-rc4+dev
commit: c964c415544c3981911bf9632e518c083ddee72b
spec: 1.0.0

```

@rhatdan and @TomSweeneyRedHat

> You did hit return after running the buildah run command, a couple of times?

Yes I did!


---

runc already up to date

```console
# dnf -y update runc --enablerepo updates-testing
Last metadata expiration check: 0:04:42 ago on Thu 28 Sep 2017 04:03:02 PM UTC.
Dependencies resolved.
Nothing to do.
Complete!
```


```console
# runc -v
runc version 1.0.0-rc4+dev
commit: 9b6aa028290012f00201d772d72f7893d96bd49d
spec: 1.0.0
```

---

```console
{"names":["chroot"],"action":"SCMP_ACT_ALLOW"},{"names":["clone"],"action":"SCMP_ACT_ALLOW",

# /bin/bash: line 1: $'\r': command not found

```

Again `buildah --debug run $ctrid_fedora /bin/bash` remains stuck, I then press `Ctrl + C` and hit return once when I get error as `/bin/bash: line 1: $'\r': command not found`.

If you run buildah with another command like

buildah run CTRID ls /

Does that hang also?

@rhatdan that works

```console
# buildah run $ctrid_fedora ls /
bin
boot
dev
etc
home
lib
lib64
lost+found
media
mnt
opt
proc
root
run
sbin
srv
sys
tmp
usr
var

```
To me this looks like something is wrong with the tty handling then.

@surajssd can you try one more test please?  After you do 

buildah run $ctrid_fedora /bin/bash

Can you try doing a number of commands?  i.e.  'ls', 'cd /var/log', 'cat README', etc.

I've just run into the same issue on my VM.  It looks like the container is up and happy, it's just not showing any kind of command prompt.

@surajssd one last test if you please.  Can you try adding the '--tty' param to the buidah run command and see if that works for you?  It does for me.
```
# buildah run --tty fedora-working-container-3 /bin/bash
[root@mrsdalloway /]# 
```
Thanks for your help with running this down.

@TomSweeneyRedHat here is what I did:

Installing on the latest master

```console
$ make
go build -ldflags '-X main.gitCommit=f6c2a1e -X main.buildInfo=1506925999' -o buildah -tags "   selinux seccomp" ./cmd/buildah
go build -ldflags '-X main.gitCommit=f6c2a1e -X main.buildInfo=1506925999' -o imgtype -tags "   selinux seccomp" ./tests/imgtype.go
make -C docs
make[1]: Entering directory '/home/vagrant/go/src/github.com/projectatomic/buildah/docs'
make[1]: Nothing to be done for 'docs'.
make[1]: Leaving directory '/home/vagrant/go/src/github.com/projectatomic/buildah/docs'
```

Using the flag `--tty` seems to work

```console
# ctrid_fedora=$(buildah from fedora)

# buildah containers list
CONTAINER ID  BUILDER  IMAGE ID     IMAGE NAME                       CONTAINER NAME
296fef349473     *     0c89760a475f docker.io/library/fedora:latest  fedora-working-container

# buildah run --tty $ctrid_fedora /bin/bash
[root@mrsdalloway /]# ls
bin  boot  dev  etc  home  lib  lib64  lost+found  media  mnt  opt  proc  root  run  sbin  srv  sys  tmp  usr  var
[root@mrsdalloway /]# pwd
/
```

But if I don't use the flag then it is stuck again.


Thanks for bearing with me!
Should we improve the man page for this use case?

@surajssd we're planning to address this issue with a documentation update which basically says "use -tty to get interactive i/o  from the container".  This is in PR #276.  Please let me know if this doesn't address your concerns.
@TomSweeneyRedHat yes, commands work, there is no cmd prompt, but commands work, which is kinda confusing!

```console
# buildah run  $ctrid_fedora /bin/bash                                                                                                                                         
ls                                             
bin                                                                                            
boot                                           
dev                                            
etc
home
lib
lib64
lost+found
media
mnt
opt
proc
root
run
sbin
srv
sys
tmp
usr
var



pwd
/

cat /etc/os-release                            
NAME=Fedora                                    
VERSION="26 (Twenty Six)"                      
ID=fedora                                      
VERSION_ID=26                                  
PRETTY_NAME="Fedora 26 (Twenty Six)"           
ANSI_COLOR="0;34"                              
CPE_NAME="cpe:/o:fedoraproject:fedora:26"      
HOME_URL="https://fedoraproject.org/"          
BUG_REPORT_URL="https://bugzilla.redhat.com/"  
REDHAT_BUGZILLA_PRODUCT="Fedora"               
REDHAT_BUGZILLA_PRODUCT_VERSION=26             
REDHAT_SUPPORT_PRODUCT="Fedora"                
REDHAT_SUPPORT_PRODUCT_VERSION=26              
PRIVACY_POLICY_URL=https://fedoraproject.org/wiki/Legal:PrivacyPolicy                          

^C
# /bin/bash: line 9: $'\r': command not found    
```
@surajssd Thanks for the confirmation.  I concur that is confusing to have the commands work without the prompt.
@surajssd as an FYI,  #276 is our proposed fix for the issue which is a documentation change only.  Thanks again for your help in alerting us to this issue and for working with us in solving it.
@TomSweeneyRedHat I am glad, I could be of help!
